definition: Freedom from arbitrary control; a political and personal good.
example: They who can give up essential liberty to obtain a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety.
source: Reply to the Governor / Pennsylvania Assembly

definition: The condition of being protected from danger.
example: They who can give up essential liberty to obtain a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety.
source: Reply to the Governor / Pennsylvania Assembly

definition: Absolutely necessary; forming the core of something.
example: They who can give up essential liberty to obtain a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety.
source: Reply to the Governor / Pennsylvania Assembly

example: Early to bed and early to rise makes a man healthy, wealthy, and wise—and industry is the engine under that proverb.

definition: The quality of being economical with money or resources.
example: Beware of little expenses; a small leak will sink a great ship—and frugality plugs the leak.

definition: Careful and persistent work or effort.
example: Diligence is the mother of good luck.

definition: A settlement of dispute by mutual concession.
example: When a broad table is to be made, and the edges of planks do not fit, the artist takes a little from both—and compromise makes the joint.

definition: The fundamental law of a political body.
example: I agree to this Constitution with all its faults, if they are such; because I think a general government necessary for us.
I agree to this Constitution with all its faults, if they are such; because I think a general government necessary for us.

definition: A modest view of one's own importance.
example: In this world nothing can be said to be certain, except death and taxes—a line that teaches humility about our plans.
source: Letter on the Constitution / later maxims

definition: Behavior showing high moral standards; excellence of character.
example: Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom. As nations become corrupt and vicious, they have more need of masters—virtue is the republic's fuel.
Energy and persistence conquer all things.

definition: Firm continuance in a course of action despite difficulty.
example: Energy and persistence conquer all things.

definition: Unable to be found or recovered; wasted.
example: Lost time is never found again.

definition: In a good or satisfactory way; thoroughly done.
example: Well done is better than well said.

definition: To be suspended; metaphorically, to stand or fall together.
example: We must, indeed, all hang together, or most assuredly we shall all hang separately.
source: At the signing of the Declaration (attributed)
